topics: 🏛️ Whose courtyard? Liability by domain access
📜 R' Shimon b. Elazar's four-rule beraita on רשות
👩 Women included in נזק despite the masculine pasuk
💰 Court appraisal: only מקרקעין, only with עדים, only with בני חורין
🐂 Mishnah 1:4 — five tam, five mu'ad; R' Tarfon's full damage in רשות הניזק

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Whose Domain, Whose Damage
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: Daf 6 walks the Mishnah's domain clauses with surgical care. R' Yirmiyah and R' Yose each show why נוֹהֲגִין הֵן בִּרְשׁוּת הַנִּיזָּק וְהַמַּזִּיק is not redundant — for one it includes the שומרים; for the other it excludes a courtyard belonging to neither. R' Yose b. Elazar's four-rule beraita then becomes the master grid for analyzing damages by domain access. The daf moves into Mishnah 1:3 (court procedure, women, joint liability) and opens Mishnah 1:4–1:5 — the count of five tam and five mu'ad, R' Tarfon's view of full damage in רשות הניזק, and which wild animals are notorious by default.
overview.hebrewSource: אַרְבָּעָה כְּלָלוֹת הָיָה רִבִּי שִׁמְעוֹן בֶּן אֶלְעָזָר אוֹמֵר מִשּׁוּם רִבִּי מֵאִיר בִּנְזָקִין

slides.title: 💬 Why נוֹהֲגִין בִּרְשׁוּת הַנִּיזָּק וְהַמַּזִּיק?
slides.subtitle: R' Yirmiyah and R' Yose split the redundancy.

lines.speaker: RY
lines.text: R' Yirmiyah: if the Mishnah only excluded the מזיק's private domain, I'd say liability applies even to a courtyard belonging to neither. So the extra clause comes to include the four שומרים.

lines.speaker: RYo
lines.text: R' Yose: that exclusion was already obvious. The extra clause comes to exclude a courtyard belonging to neither.

lines.speaker: T1
lines.text: Some Tannaim: shared courtyard is liable; courtyard of neither is exempt — מֵיטַב שָׂדֵהוּ.

lines.speaker: T2
lines.text: Some Tannaim: even a courtyard of neither is liable — וּבִעֵר בִּשְׂדֵה אַחֵר. מִכָּל מָקוֹם.

callout.label: Yerushalmi style:
callout.text: Two amoraim disagree about what the same Mishnah clause is doing. Each reading then aligns with a different tannaitic camp.

slides.title: 📊 R' Shimon b. Elazar's Four Rules
slides.subtitle: אַרְבָּעָה כְּלָלוֹת בִּנְזָקִין — the master grid for liability by domain.

callout.label: Why this grid matters:
callout.text: R' Yasa b'shem R' Yochanan derives that חצר השותפין חייבת precisely from this beraita — line 1 already obligates damages even though both have access.

slides.title: 💰 Mishnah 1:3 — Court Procedure
slides.subtitle: Each phrase narrows the appraisal procedure.

steps.content: 1️⃣ שָׁוֶה כֶסֶף — only mortgageable property (קרקע) is appraised. (Movables only if נִזָּק seized them.)
2️⃣ בִּפְנֵי בֵית דִּין — only in court.
3️⃣ עַל פִּי עֵדִים — only with witnesses.
4️⃣ בְּנֵי חוֹרִין וּבְנֵי בְרִית — excludes Gentiles, slaves, and those disqualified for testimony.
5️⃣ וְהַנָּשִׁים בִּכְלָל הַנֶּזֶק — women are in the system; R' Yishmael: וְאֵלֶּה הַמִּשְׁפָּטִים אֲשֶׁר תָּשִׂים לִפְנֵיהֶם (the verse must explicitly include them).

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: וְהַנִּיזָּק וְהַמַּזִּיק בְּתַשְׁלוּמִין — when both pay, each pays half. זֶה מַפְסִיד חֲצִי נֶזֶק וְזֶה מַפְסִיד חֲצִי נֶזֶק.

slides.title: 🐂 Mishnah 1:4 — Five Tam, Five Mu'ad
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah charts the default temperaments.

boxes.title: ⚖️ R' Tarfon
items: R' Yochanan: our Mishnah is R' Tarfon's

boxes.title: 🦁 Mishnah 1:5
items: Wolf, lion, bear, tiger, panther, snake — mu'ad
R' Eliezer: trained ones aren't mu'ad — except snake
R' Yose b'R' Avin: only male hyena, dangerous as a lion

callout.label: The thread:
callout.text: Tam vs. mu'ad isn't a static label — it tracks behavior. The Mishnah's five-and-five frame is then immediately complicated by R' Tarfon, R' Eliezer, and R' Meir.

slides.title: 🔎 Whose Courtyard? Decision Map
slides.subtitle: Putting R' Shimon b. Elazar's grid to work.

columns.label: Neither's
columns.content: Like public space — שן/רגל exempt; קרן at half/full

nodes.content: R' Yasa: from line 1 alone we learn חֲצַר הַשּׁוּתָפִין חַיֶיבֶת.

callout.label: Practical reading:
callout.text: Domain access is the master variable. Each row of the beraita is a different liability profile, and the Mishnah's clauses are designed to land precisely in this grid.

summary.principle: Domain access defines liability, and each clause of the Mishnah is hand-fitted to the four-cell grid of R' Shimon b. Elazar's beraita.

flowSteps.content:
Mishnah's clauses on domain
חוץ מרשות המיוחדת למזיק

Why is נוהגין ברשות הניזק והמזיק needed?
R' Yirmiyah → שומרים; R' Yose → exclude neither's

🎓 Mishnah 1:3–1:5 follow:
Court appraisal procedure → women included in tort liability → five tam and five mu'ad → wild animals' default mu'ad status. The daf weaves procedure, status, and substance into one map.

quiz.q: Why does R' Yirmiyah think the Mishnah needs נוֹהֲגִין הֵן בִּרְשׁוּת הַנִּיזָּק וְהַמַּזִּיק?
o: To exclude a courtyard belonging to neither
To include the four שומרים (unpaid trustee, borrower, paid trustee, renter)
To require both parties to be present
quiz.e: R' Yirmiyah reads the extra clause as bringing in the שומרים — שומר חינם, שואל, נושא שכר, שוכר.

quiz.q: What does R' Yose think the extra clause is doing?
o: Excluding a courtyard belonging to neither party
quiz.e: R' Yose: the מזיק's private exclusion was obvious, so the clause must be excluding חצר שאינה לשניהם.

quiz.q: According to R' Shimon b. Elazar's grid, what is the law for שן/רגל damage in a shared courtyard?
quiz.e: עַל הַשֵּׁן וְעַל הָרֶגֶל פָּטוּר when both have access — only קרן is liable, half-tam or full-mu'ad.

quiz.q: What does R' Yishmael's drash וְאֵלֶּה הַמִּשְׁפָּטִים אֲשֶׁר תָּשִׂים לִפְנֵיהֶם establish?
o: That courts must convene with three judges
That women are included in נזק liability — both as victims and perpetrators
That property must be appraised in court
quiz.e: Since the verse only addresses men, R' Yishmael needs לִפְנֵיהֶם to include women in the tort framework.

quiz.q: Whose view does R' Yochanan say underlies our Mishnah's count of five mu'ad — including שור המזיק ברשות הניזק?
o: R' Tarfon, who says full damage in רשות הניזק
quiz.e: אָמַר רִבִּי יוֹחָנָן. דְּרִבִּי טַרְפוֹן הִיא — R' Tarfon obligates full damage in the נִזָּק's domain; the Sages say half.
